equalizer_filter_list_no_filter_export_a: Please add at least one filter before exporting.
equalizer_autoeq_description: AutoEQ will use as many filters as available.
equalizer_device_peq_incompatible_browse: Your browser does not support the necessary APIs to connect to devices. Please use a compatible browser (e.g. Chrome or Edge) and ensure you have the required permissions enabled.
equalizer_device_peq_info_overview_intro: Device PEQ lets you read and write parametric EQ filters directly to supported audio hardware from the browser. No extra software required — the tool talks to the device over the Web USB, Serial, Bluetooth, and Network APIs.
equalizer_device_peq_info_overview_hid: USB HID — plug-and-play via WebHID for DAC/amps like FiiO, Moondrop, and KTMicro dongles.
equalizer_device_peq_info_overview_seria: USB Serial — for devices exposing a CDC / virtual COM interface (JDS Labs, Nothing, EarFun, and more).
equalizer_device_peq_info_overview_ble: Bluetooth — wireless pairing via Web Bluetooth for select FiiO and Airoha-chipset devices.
equalizer_device_peq_info_overview_netwo: Network — IP-addressable devices such as WiiM streamers and Luxsin X9.
equalizer_device_peq_info_howto_step_1: Pick the connection type that matches your device (USB HID, USB Serial, Bluetooth, or Network).
equalizer_device_peq_info_howto_step_2: Approve the device in the browser's permission prompt. For Network devices, enter the device IP address.
equalizer_device_peq_info_howto_step_3: Once connected, choose the EQ slot you want to read from or write to.
equalizer_device_peq_info_howto_step_4: Use Pull from Device to load the current filters into the equalizer, or Push to Device to write the current filters back.
equalizer_device_peq_info_howto_step_5: Click Disconnect when you're done. Some devices will auto-disconnect after a successful write.
equalizer_device_peq_info_howto_note: Requires a Chromium-based browser (Chrome, Edge, Opera) with WebHID, Web Serial, and/or Web Bluetooth enabled.
tutorial_modal_menu_content: You can slide the menu to the left or right to access the variety of tools.
tutorial_modal_graph_handle_content: You can drag this handle to shift graph vertically. Double click to reset position.
tutorial_modal_divider_content: You can drag this divider to change the size of the graph.
tutorial_modal_shortcuts_content: Use keyboard shortcuts to speed up your workflow. You can find the list of shortcuts at the bottom left corner.
tutorial_modal_pwa_content: modernGraphTool is built as a progressive web app (PWA). You can install it to your device for full-app experience.
tutorial_modal_pwa_inst_ios: On Safari: Tap the Share button, then select 'Add to Home Screen'.
tutorial_modal_pwa_inst_android: On Chrome: Tap the menu button (three dots), then select 'Install app' or 'Add to Home screen'.
tutorial_modal_guide_content: If you're new to frequency response graphs or modernGraphTool, our user guide walks you through the basics.
tutorial_freq_sub_bass_desc: The Rumble, usually out of human's hearing range and tend to be felt more than heard, providing a sense of power.
tutorial_freq_bass_desc: Determines how 'fat' or 'thin' the sound is. Boosting around 250 Hz tends to add warmth. If you're a bass head you most likely like this range.
tutorial_freq_lower_mids_desc: Low order harmonics of most instruments, generally viewed as the bass presence range. Boosting around 300 Hz adds clarity to the bass. Too much around 500 Hz can make higher-frequency instruments sound muffled.
tutorial_freq_upper_mids_desc: The high midrange is responsible for the attack on percussive and rhythm instruments. Boosting this range can add presence. However, too much around 3 kHz can cause listening fatigue.
tutorial_freq_presence_desc: The presence range is responsible for the clarity and definition of a sound. Over-boosting can cause an irritating, harsh sound. Cutting here makes the sound more distant and transparent.
tutorial_freq_brilliance_desc: The brilliance range is composed entirely of harmonics and is responsible for sparkle and air of a sound. Over-boosting can accentuate hiss and cause ear fatigue.
eq_settings_link_eq_normalization_descri: Keep the EQ curve positioned relative to the original graph regardless of normalization settings.
